title: Methods should not perform too many tasks (Brain method)
description: A method that performs too many tasks is often referred to as a 'Brain method'. Such methods are difficult to understand, maintain, and test. They tend to centralize too much logic, making the code less modular and more prone to errors.
recommendation: Refactor large methods into smaller, well-named methods that each perform a single, well-defined task. This improves readability, maintainability, and testability.
impact: High cognitive complexity in methods makes code harder to maintain and increases the risk of defects.

checks.message: Method is too long or does too much (brain method).
